dry_bulk.confluence_note: A stark contrast exists between expansionary spot pricing (BDI z=+1.97) and persistently negative fundamental assessments driven by record Chinese iron ore inventories.
dry_bulk.summary: Spot rates have hit 2-month highs, pushing the Capesize and Panamax sectors into the 99th percentile of 5-year historical values. However, weak Chinese steel production (-3.6% YoY) and bloated portside stocks (166mt) suggest the current rally lacks a structural demand foundation.
dry_bulk.outlook: Cautiously bearish on long-term sustainability despite short-term momentum.
dry_bulk.watch: Chinese steel production YTD figures and iron ore portside inventory depletion rates.

tanker.confluence_note: Quantitative metrics and qualitative fundamentals are perfectly aligned, with clean tankers showing an extreme z-score of +3.28.
tanker.summary: Geopolitical conflict and unnavigable straits have triggered a massive explosion in rates, with Clean Tankers posting a +150.1% 60-day rate of change. Sustained positive sentiment and fundamentals reflect a market struggling with supply disruptions and shifting trade routes as the Middle East conflict enters its second month. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2123.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2831.0 (ROC60 +78.3%).
key_signals: Global Crude Oil On Water -0.4% (Inventories Drawing)
tanker.outlook: Strongly bullish as long as geopolitical instability maintains trade route disruptions.
tanker.watch: Navigability of key straits and Middle East war escalation markers.

macro_note: The shipping market is bifurcated; tankers are pricing in a major geopolitical supply shock, while dry bulk is experiencing a momentum-driven rally that faces significant headwinds from a slowing Chinese industrial sector.
